Legacy/source details:
**Score:** 7.4/10 | **Revenue target:** $3K–$10K/mo | **Build time:** 2 weeks via coding agent

**The Opportunity:**
WalkthroughProof turns move-in/move-out apartment photos into a timestamped condition ledger, checklist, and PDF/ZIP evidence packet. Renters already take photos, but they rarely organize them into something useful before a deposit dispute. The product works autonomously because fulfillment is upload → AI triage → user review → Stripe unlock → PDF/ZIP export.

**Stack:**
Next.js + Vercel, Supabase Postgres + Storage or Cloudflare R2, Gemini/OpenAI vision, Claude neutral-description layer, React PDF/Puppeteer PDF generator, Stripe Checkout, PostHog/analytics, OpenClaw weekly SEO/demo-content cron.

**Build Steps (Phase 1 MVP):**
1. Create/clone a minimal Next.js app named `walkthroughproof` with `/new-report`.
2. Build address optional + move-in/move-out toggle + room list + upload up to 30 compressed photos.
3. Store photo metadata, timestamps, file hashes, room labels, and original filenames in Supabase/R2.
4. Build `analyze-photo-batch`: vision model returns room, visible issue candidates, confidence, and checklist prompts; Claude rewrites into neutral descriptions.
5. Build review UI where the user edits/accepts each finding before export.
6. Generate PDF + ZIP with cover page, room checklist, defect ledger, source-photo appendix, and hash/timestamp table.
7. Add Stripe: free preview with watermark/limited findings; $9 unlocks full PDF/ZIP.

**Monetization:**
- First $1: user pays $9 after preview to unlock the full PDF/ZIP evidence packet.
- Pricing: free preview, $9 one-off report, $19/mo renter vault, $49/mo small-landlord branded reports, later $2–$5/report API.
- Path to $3K/mo: 250 one-off reports × $9 = $2,250 + 40 renter vault subs × $19 = $760.

**Marketing Strategy:**
- Primary channel: SEO + renter-community proof demos.
- Week 1 launch: Reddit in r/Apartmentliving/r/Renters format: “I got tired of move-in photos living in a camera roll, so I built a report generator for deposit disputes — roast the checklist.”
- Ongoing: weekly SEO pages and 15–30s before/after demos: camera roll → defect ledger → PDF.
- SEO: move in inspection checklist photos, security deposit photo proof, rental inspection report app, apartment walkthrough checklist, move out condition report.
- Viral mechanism: shareable report summary screenshot before move-out/moving season.

**Automation:**
- User uploads → model groups/labels → Claude drafts neutral findings → user reviews → Stripe unlock → PDF/ZIP generated → report emailed/stored.
- OpenClaw cron needed: yes — `walkthroughproof-content` after launch to generate checklist posts, SEO refreshes, and anonymized demo captions.

**Full blueprint:** memory/passive-income/2026-05-13-strategist.md
title: [PI] Build: WalkthroughProof — renter move-in photo evidence packets

Legacy/source details:
**Score:** 7.3/10 | **Source:** Greg Isenberg agent-business thread, 2026-05-10 | **Why it fits JT:** this is basically JT’s Spectrum + Aya + Altmark edge: find the spreadsheet the business lives in, turn it into an exception dashboard/agent desk.

**Concept:** A repeatable micro-SaaS/template that ingests one messy operational spreadsheet and creates an agent desk: status, exceptions, stale rows, owner, next action, weekly summary, approval queue.

**Best wedge:** property-management rent/maintenance tracker, construction job tracker, or wholesale order-status sheet.

**First validation action:** Use an anonymized Aya/Altmark-style sheet shape and create a demo “before spreadsheet → after agent desk” screenshot/video.

**MVP build:**
1. Define canonical CSV schema adapter: row id, owner, date, status, amount/address, notes, next action.
2. Build Next.js upload/demo page.
3. Claude/n8n classifies exceptions and drafts owner updates.
4. Dashboard shows only stuck/changed/approval-needed items.
5. Export weekly owner report.

**Monetization:** $99 one-time spreadsheet audit, $49/mo hosted dashboard, or consulting lead magnet into $2K–$10K implementation.

**Done state:** demo live + one real spreadsheet converted.

**Risk:** This is more service-productized than passive at first. Only keep in Passive $ if the schema/template becomes reusable across 3+ businesses.
title: [PI] Validate: Spreadsheet-to-Agent Desk — productize shadow spreadsheet replacement

Legacy/source details:
**Score:** 7.4/10 | **Revenue target:** $3K–$10K/mo | **Build time:** 2–4 weeks via coding agent

**The Opportunity:**
Photo-first collection organizer for vinyl/board-game collectors. Turns shelf photos into normalized inventory, duplicates, estimated replacement value, and insurance-ready CSV/PDF. Strongest wedge is one API-rich vertical first, not every collectible category.

**Stack:**
Next.js + Vercel + Supabase/Postgres + S3/R2 image storage + Gemini/OpenAI vision + Claude resolver + Discogs or BoardGameGeek API + Stripe + OpenClaw/n8n value-refresh cron.

**Build Steps (Phase 1 MVP):**
1. Pick launch vertical: vinyl recommended because Discogs metadata is strongest.
2. Build upload flow for shelf photo + close-up photo + optional Discogs CSV import.
3. Create vision extraction queue for OCR/title/artist/year/edition candidates.
4. Resolve against Discogs API with confidence scores and manual confirmation UI.
5. Build dashboard with confirmed items, duplicates, value band, CSV/PDF export.
6. Add Stripe: $19 one-time shelf inventory capped by item/photo limit; $12/mo monthly value refresh.
7. Deploy and verify upload → extract → confirm → export → test payment.

**Monetization:**
- First $1: collector pays $19 to process a shelf batch and export inventory PDF/CSV.
- Pricing: free 5-item demo scan, $19 batch, $12/mo value refresh, $49 large collection pack.
- Path to $3K/mo: 100 batch purchases × $19 = $1.9K + 100 subscribers × $12 = $1.2K.

**Marketing Strategy:**
- Primary channel: collector community before/after demos + SEO.
- Week 1 launch: r/vinyl — “I built a tool that turns shelf photos into a Discogs-style inventory — beta testers wanted.”
- Ongoing: agent posts before/after inventory clips, SEO templates, and collector-organization answers.
- SEO: vinyl collection organizer, Discogs photo scanner, collectible insurance inventory, board game collection organizer.
- Viral mechanism: shareable collection value summary and organized collection page.

**Automation:**
- User uploads → vision extracts candidates → resolver matches metadata → user confirms → CSV/PDF exports → monthly value refresh.
- OpenClaw cron needed: yes — value refresh + anonymized before/after content generator.

**Full blueprint:** memory/passive-income/2026-05-10-strategist.md
title: [PI] Build: CollectionProof — photo-to-insurance inventory for collectors

description:
First action: review this idea against current North Star priority and either select a single validation action, merge it into an existing app/client system, or park it.

Why it matters: passive-income ideas are useful only when they become measured app distribution, reusable IP, or a clear revenue test; otherwise they add backlog noise.

Done state: build/validate/defer/kill is recorded; if selected, this idea is replaced by one scoped implementation task with repo/path, metric, and owner.

---
Legacy/source details:
**Score:** 7.8/10 | **Revenue target:** $3K–$10K/mo | **Build time:** 1–2 weeks via coding agent

**The Opportunity:**
Focused skincare decision engine for the PDRN/salmon-DNA boom. Ranks products by ingredient credibility, claim risk, sensitive-skin fit, format, and price-per-active; monetizes through affiliate links and $9 routine-fit reports. Build as Ingredient Decoder with PDRN as the first vertical, so future skincare micro-trends can reuse the engine.

**Stack:**
Next.js + Vercel/Replit + SQLite/Postgres + Claude/Gemini scoring script + Stripe + affiliate links + OpenClaw/n8n weekly ingestion cron.

**Build Steps (Phase 1 MVP):**
1. Clone Glow Index ranking pattern into an ingredient-decoder app; create Product, Claim, Format, SkinGoal, RetailerLink entities.
2. Seed 40–60 PDRN products from major retailers with INCI text, claims, format, price, and affiliate URL.
3. Build scoring script to classify claim risk, irritation risk, format fit, price-per-active, and recommendation tags.
4. Build SEO pages: /pdrn, /pdrn/best-serums, /pdrn/cream-vs-serum, /pdrn/sensitive-skin, product pages.
5. Add Stripe $9 routine-fit report and verify deploy + test payment + affiliate disclosures.

**Monetization:**
- First $1: $9 Stripe routine-fit report or affiliate click from ranked product page.
- Pricing: free rankings, $9 one-time report, later $3 product claim-risk JSON API.
- Path to $3K/mo: 15K monthly SEO visitors × 2% report conversion × $9 = $2.7K + affiliate.

**Marketing Strategy:**
- Primary channel: SEO for PDRN product/format/skin-type queries.
- Week 1 launch: r/AsianBeauty or r/SkincareAddiction — “I built a PDRN claim-risk score because every product page says the same thing — roast the scoring logic.”
- Ongoing: agent publishes weekly product comparisons, claim-risk snippets, and long-tail ingredient pages.
- SEO: best PDRN serum, PDRN cream vs serum, PDRN sensitive skin, salmon DNA serum.
- Viral mechanism: shareable “PDRN Claim Risk Card” from reports.

**Automation:**
- Weekly product discovery + scoring draft cron.
- Report purchases generate constrained markdown/PDF automatically.
- OpenClaw cron needed: yes — `ingredient-decoder-ingest` for new products + SEO/social drafts.

**Full blueprint:** memory/passive-income/2026-05-10-strategist.md
title: [PI] Build: PDRN Decoder — turn PDRN skincare hype into ranked buying decisions

Legacy/source details:
First action: Run `openclaw --version` to check current version. If < 2026.5.1, run `openclaw update` or reinstall via npm/brew. Then verify Codex ACP harness starts correctly: spawn a test session with `sessions_spawn` using runtime="acp" and agentId for Codex.
Why now: OpenClaw 2026.5.1 shipped critical fixes: (1) Codex/app-server now resolves managed binaries from bundled dist chunks — fixes false "missing binary" startup failures that could break JT's Codex ACP harness. (2) Gateway/CLI repairs stale managed service definitions before starting. (3) ACPX and OpenTelemetry externalized to separate packages — reduces core bloat. (4) 20+ plugins prepped for beta (Google Chat, LINE, Matrix, Discord, WhatsApp, Brave, Codex, etc.) — signals major ecosystem expansion.
Expected outcome: OpenClaw updated to 2026.5.1; Codex ACP harness verified working; no stale service definitions

Source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases (official, 2 days ago) + https://releasebot.io/updates/openclaw (8 hours ago)
Relevance: OpenClaw — JT's primary agent platform; Codex ACP harness
Cost: free (update)
Fits: Eve direct action / OpenClaw maintenance

Demoted by daily North Star audit: useful, but not immediate next-dollar/proof/distribution unless tied to an active opportunity.

Legacy/source details:
**AWS Bedrock / Amazon Q: BedrockBridge — Enterprise RAG Agent on AWS**
Skill being developed: AWS Bedrock (GenAI/agentic platform) + Amazon Q — now appearing in 3+ NYC enterprise AI consulting JDs as a named requirement, distinct from AWS SageMaker (ML training). Bedrock is the agentic/GenAI stack: knowledge bases, Claude/Titan models, agents, guardrails.
What to build: A working RAG agent built entirely on AWS Bedrock services: (1) create an AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base ingesting a sample JT Somwaru Consulting client document set (PDF/HTML → vector store via Bedrock's managed OpenSearch Serverless), (2) build a Bedrock Agent that uses the knowledge base + a Lambda action group to answer structured queries (e.g., "summarize this client's pain points"), (3) deploy a simple interface (API Gateway → Lambda → Bedrock Agent), (4) document architecture diagram + README comparing Bedrock vs. OpenAI Assistants API vs. LangChain RAG approaches — the "three-way comparison" is the portfolio differentiator. Optional: layer in Bedrock Guardrails to demonstrate responsible AI content filtering.
Portfolio value: Directly addresses the AWS Bedrock requirement now appearing in enterprise AI architect and consulting JDs (Glassdoor NYC AI consulting listings, Schonfeld-adjacent roles, GEP Agentic AI). Shows JT can architect on the AWS AI stack, not just Salesforce/OpenAI. Resume talking point: "Built an enterprise RAG agent on AWS Bedrock with knowledge base ingestion, Lambda action groups, and Guardrails — the same stack used by Fortune 500 firms migrating internal knowledge to agentic AI." Also positions JT for cross-cloud consulting (Microsoft Copilot + Salesforce Agentforce + AWS Bedrock = full enterprise AI platform fluency).
Estimated build time: 1–2 days via Claude Code (AWS free tier + Bedrock pay-per-use ~$5–$10)
Date generated: 2026-02-28
Status: idea

Source: job-market-agent daily scan

The output format should mirror what a McKinsey/Deloitte AI practice would produce. Build it, use it for a real Aya or JT Somwaru Consulting client, and document it as a portfolio case study.

Shows JT can own the strategic layer of AI transformation, not just implementation. This is the skill gap that separates a hands-on consultant from an advisory-level role commanding $180K+. Use this as both a client deliverable and an interview demo: "I built the tool I use to scope my own client engagements." Aligns with the market shift per Salesforce Ben: employers want people who know "when and why" to deploy AI, not just how.

Recommendation: use sandboxed GBrain only for consulting entity lookup for now; hold broad adoption.

---
Legacy/source details:
## Built (Completed)

*(Move here when JT builds something from this list)*

**Model Context Protocol / Multi-Agent Interoperability: AgentMesh — Agentforce Cross-Platform Interoperability Demo**
Skill being developed: Model Context Protocol (MCP) + Multi-Agent Interoperability — now explicitly required in the AI-201 Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist exam (2026 update). AI-201 now tests "MCP-based tool-calling" and "Multi-Agent Interoperability" as exam domains. Not in JT's profile beyond conceptual awareness. This is the emerging standard for how Agentforce agents call external AI tools and how agents from different vendors collaborate.
What to build: A working POC where an Agentforce agent orchestrates external tools via an MCP server: (1) Build a simple MCP server (using FastMCP or the Anthropic MCP SDK) that exposes 2–3 tools — e.g., a Claude-powered deep analysis tool, a web search tool, and a document summarizer; (2) Configure an Agentforce agent to call these external tools via the MCP protocol (using Agentforce's External Services + Apex callout or the upcoming native MCP support); (3) Demo scenario: Agentforce Service Agent receives a complex customer inquiry → delegates to the MCP-hosted Claude analysis tool → returns enriched response; (4) Write a 1-page "When Agentforce needs an external brain: MCP integration pattern" explainer documenting when native Agentforce actions are sufficient vs. when to call out to external agents via MCP. Note: JT already has FastMCP experience from JT Somwaru Consulting Cowork plugin work — apply that knowledge to the Agentforce context.

Legacy/source details:
HubSpot MCP Insurance Agency Pipeline Demo package is built at ~/projects/jt-consulting-pipeline/clients/hubspot-mcp-insurance-demo/

**Key callout:** Fastest demo path is Claude-MCP only — zero n8n required. Just HubSpot connector active in Claude Desktop + 3 prompts:
1. "Summarize my HubSpot deals with no activity in 5+ days past close date"
2. "Create follow-up tasks on each of these deals"
3. Show tasks appearing live in HubSpot

**Full package includes:**
- brief.json: ICP targeting, 3-step demo flow, screencast script (7 time-stamped segments)
- workflow.json: 5-node n8n workflow (Webhook + HTTP + IF + HubSpot + Telegram) — premium add-on layer
- HANDOFF.md: step-by-step config guide, 3 exact prompts, 6 v2 ideas

**Prerequisites needed from JT:**
1. HubSpot free/trial account seeded with 15 contacts + 8 deals (5+ days stale)
2. Claude Desktop with HubSpot MCP connector installed
3. Record 5-min screencast using screencast_script from brief.json

Deliverable: 5-min screencast → ready for prospect presentations at $2,500-3,500

description:
First action: open `~/projects/job-market-agent/data/agent-ideas.md`, find this idea, and validate whether it directly supports a current target role or consulting proof before building.

Why it matters: job-market build ideas should close a real positioning gap, not create an unbounded portfolio backlog.

Done state: decision is recorded as build/defer/kill; if build, a scoped implementation task with repo/path and success metric replaces this idea.

---
Legacy/source details:
Skill being developed: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol (Google's open standard for async task delegation between AI agents — GA Jan 2026. Explicitly required in Microsoft AB-100 exam scope. Pairs with AgentMesh/MCP to create full multi-agent interoperability coverage.)
What to build: A working demo where an Agentforce agent delegates a research subtask to an external Claude-based agent using the A2A protocol: (1) Agentforce service agent receives a complex customer inquiry, (2) spawns a task via A2A to a Claude-based research agent running on a simple FastAPI server, (3) Claude agent completes async research, returns structured result via A2A task completion event, (4) Agentforce agent incorporates the result and responds to the customer, (5) Write a 1-page comparison: "A2A vs. MCP — when to delegate vs. when to call a tool."
Portfolio value: Covers the exact gap in Microsoft's AB-100 exam scope. Positions JT for the coming wave of multi-vendor enterprise agentic architectures. 1-day build reusing FastAPI + Agentforce setup from AgentMesh.

20-minute task — highest consulting SEO ROI per minute. Shows up in local NYC search within days.

Step-by-step:
1. Go to https://business.google.com — sign in with your Google account
2. Click "Manage now" → Business name: JT Somwaru Consulting
3. Category: Business Management Consultant (primary) + Information Technology Consultant (secondary)
4. Service-area business (no storefront) → Service area: New York, NY metro
5. Phone + website: https://jtsomwaru.com
6. Verify via phone/text (instant — not the 2-week postcard)
7. Once verified, add this description exactly: "AI implementation specialist helping NYC businesses in construction, property management, insurance, and wholesale distribution automate operations with n8n and Salesforce Agentforce. 6 years enterprise ops background at Spectrum Enterprise."
8. Add services: n8n Workflow Automation ($3,500) | Agentforce Implementation ($6,500) | AI App Development ($4,500)
9. Add your headshot photo
10. Set business hours

Why it matters: Appears in local map pack above organic results for "AI consultant NYC" and "automation consultant New York" searches. Free. Starts ranking within days.

1. eventbrite.com/d/ny--new-york/ — search "construction tech", "property management", "AI business"
2. brooklynchamber.com/events
3. nycchamber.com/events
